summary: McKenzie shares a brief update on the 30 Day Sprint challenge, pointing readers to a fellow participant's summary chart of all participants. He outlines his own modest agenda for the evening: watching BSG, setting up a Rails environment, and building a first proof-of-concept RSS widget.
key_points: Community collaboration in the 30 Day Sprint challenge, with participants tracking each other's progress
Starting with the simplest possible proof of concept — an RSS feed widget — rather than overengineering
The value of others doing organizational work you planned to do yourself, freeing time for building
money_quotes: I love it when people do things I was meaning to get to… less time typing means more time…. typing.
key_insight: Start with the simplest possible proof of concept and let community members handle organizational overhead so you can focus on building.
